subject: Product Management
title: MoviePass
description: A fast breakdown of MoviePass for builders.

hook.text: MoviePass offered unlimited movies for $9.95/month. It was the best deal in entertainment and the worst business model.

buildup.text: CEO Mitch Lowe believed data on moviegoers would be worth more than the cost of subsidizing tickets. 3 million people signed up.

discovery.text: The math never worked. The average user saw 1.7 movies monthly. MoviePass paid $12 per ticket. They lost money on every customer.

twist.text: MoviePass kept changing terms, blocking popular movies, and restricting usage. The 'unlimited' promise evaporated month by month.

climax.text: The company burned through $40 million per month and filed for bankruptcy. Growth without unit economics is just organized loss.

punchline.text: A great deal for users isn't a great product if you can't survive.

quiz.question: Why did MoviePass fail?
options: Unit economics never worked — they lost money on every customer
Not enough people were interested in movies
Movie theaters refused to accept MoviePass
